title: A Typical DNase I Reaction (M0303)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6gadc","text":"This is a protocol for a typical DNase I Reaction, using the M0303 RNase-free DNase I.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"dpls0","text":"When using RNA in downstream applications, column purification with NEB's Monarch RNA Cleanup Kits (NEB #T2030, #T2040, #T2050), or cleanup with phenol/chloroform extraction is recommended instead of heat inactivation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":104,"length":6},{"key":1,"offset":112,"length":6},{"key":2,"offset":120,"length":6}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"53ED32E024094493A376CA59391A55B8","url":"https://www.neb.com/products/t2030-monarch-rna-cleanup-kit-10-ug"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"B23F5DBFE369421B9A1DB6BF3CA678B7","url":"https://www.neb.com/products/t2040-monarch-rna-cleanup-kit-50-ug"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"F3AAE30857AC434E947D7FB5F3642EBF","url":"https://www.neb.com/products/t2050-monarch-rna-cleanup-kit-500-ug"}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"9fg27","text":"Please see SDS (Safety Data Sheet) for hazards and safety warnings.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Set up the following reaction On ice : A B 1 COMPONENTS 100 μl REACTION 2 RNA ~ 10 μg RNA 3 DNase I Reaction Buffer (10X) 10 μl (1X) 4 DNAse I (RNase-free) 1 μl (2 units) 5 Nuclease-free H2O to 100 μl
Add 1 µL of 0.5 Molarity (M), EDTA (to a final concentration of 5 millimolar (mM) ).
creator: New England Biolabs Tech Support
